data.title: Kit (ConvertKit): Best Email Tips & Ways to Grow Your Audience
data.excerpt: Discover how Kit (ConvertKit) helps creators build lists, automate emails, and monetize audiences. Try the free plan today and see the difference.

$media.alt: Kit (ConvertKit): Best Email Tips & Ways to Grow Your Audience

children.text: If you're a creator — a writer, coach, podcaster, course builder, or YouTuber — you already know the deal: your email list is the most valuable asset you own. Social platforms throttle your reach. Algorithms shift overnight. But your inbox? That's a direct line to the people who chose to hear from you.

The problem? Most email platforms weren't built for creators. They were built for e-commerce stores or enterprise sales teams. You end up paying for features you'll never touch while struggling to do basic things like tagging subscribers based on their interests or sending a welcome sequence that actually feels human.
That's exactly why
Kit (formerly ConvertKit)
exists. It was designed from day one for people who make things — and it shows in every feature, every workflow, and every pricing tier.
I've spent serious time inside Kit. Below, I'm sharing the best tips and ways to use it so you can squeeze every drop of value from the platform, whether you have 50 subscribers or 50,000.
*Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through them, I may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*
Why Kit Is the Email Platform Creators Actually Love

Let's get something out of the way: Kit isn't trying to be everything to everyone. It's not a CRM. It's not a sales funnel builder with 47 dashboards. It's an email marketing platform built specifically for creators, and that focus is its superpower.
Here's what that means in practice:
Subscriber-centric tagging, not list-centric folders.

In Kit, a single subscriber can live across multiple tags and segments without being duplicated or double-charged. You tag someone as "interested in productivity" AND "bought Course A" and then send hyper-targeted emails to either group — or the overlap. Most legacy platforms charge you per list, meaning the same person counts three times if they're on three lists. Kit doesn't play that game.
Visual automations that make sense.

The drag-and-drop automation builder lets you map out entire customer journeys — from lead magnet download to welcome sequence to pitch — on a single visual canvas. You can see exactly where every subscriber is in the flow. No guessing. No spaghetti code.
Built-in monetization.

You can sell digital products, subscriptions, and paid newsletters directly through Kit without bolting on a third-party checkout. Payments process through Kit's commerce tools, and the revenue shows up in your dashboard alongside open rates and click rates.
Deliverability that earns its reputation.

Kit maintains strong sender reputation across major ISPs because they actively enforce anti-spam policies. Your emails land in inboxes, not junk folders. For a creator whose livelihood depends on people actually reading their emails, this isn't a minor detail — it's everything.
If you want a deeper walkthrough of the platform's fundamentals, check out our guide on
what Kit (ConvertKit) is and why it matters
How Kit's Visual Automations Save You Hours Every Week
This is the feature that converts skeptics into loyal Kit users.
Picture this: someone downloads your free PDF guide on meal prepping. In Kit, you build an automation that:
1. Adds the tag "meal-prep-lead" to their profile.
2. Sends Email 1 immediately — the PDF link plus a warm welcome.
3. Waits 2 days, then sends Email 2 with a personal story about your own meal prep journey.
4. Waits 3 more days, then sends Email 3 pitching your paid meal prep course.
5. If they buy, remove the tag "meal-prep-lead" and add "meal-prep-customer." If they don't buy, add them to a weekly newsletter segment.

The entire flow lives on one screen. You drag nodes, connect them with lines, set wait times, and define if/then branches. It takes maybe 15 minutes to build, and then it runs forever in the background while you focus on creating.
Here's a tip most new Kit users miss:
use the "Visual Automation" entry points strategically.

You can trigger an automation when someone subscribes to a form, gets tagged, purchases a product, or even when a custom field changes. That last one is powerful. Imagine changing a subscriber's "experience level" field from "beginner" to "intermediate" after they complete a quiz — and Kit automatically moves them into an advanced email sequence.
Another pro move:
duplicate your best-performing automation and tweak it for a new audience segment.

Don't rebuild from scratch every time. Kit lets you clone entire automations, so your proven welcome sequence for podcast listeners can become a slightly modified welcome sequence for YouTube viewers in minutes.
Want a step-by-step walkthrough? Our
how-to guide for Kit (ConvertKit)
covers building your first automation from zero.
Build Your List Fast with Landing Pages and Forms That Convert
You don't need a website to start collecting subscribers with Kit. The platform includes unlimited landing pages and forms on every plan — including the free one.

Here's why that matters: a lot of email tools gate their landing page builder behind a paid tier, or they limit you to a handful of templates that look like they were designed in 2012. Kit gives you clean, modern templates that are mobile-responsive by default and load fast.
Landing Page Tips That Work
Keep it ruthlessly simple.

The highest-converting Kit landing pages have one headline, one sentence of supporting text, an email input field, and a button. No navigation menu. No sidebar. No distractions. Kit's templates are built with this philosophy baked in.
Use the "incentive email" feature.

When someone signs up through your form, Kit can automatically send them a delivery email with your lead magnet. This is technically a double opt-in flow, but it feels seamless to the subscriber and it dramatically improves deliverability because you're confirming real email addresses.
Create dedicated landing pages for each traffic source.

Have a specific page for your Instagram bio link, a different one for your podcast show notes, and another for guest post bylines. Kit lets you create as many as you want, and you can tag subscribers differently based on which page they came from. That way, your Instagram audience gets a slightly different welcome sequence than your podcast audience — because they discovered you in a different context.
A/B test subject lines.

Kit's Creator plan includes subject line A/B testing on broadcasts. Send Version A to 15% of your list, Version B to another 15%, and Kit automatically sends the winner to the remaining 70% after a set time window. This one feature alone can bump your open rates by 10-20% over a few months of consistent testing.
For a comprehensive list of ideas on what to build and send, explore our article on
Kit (ConvertKit) use cases
What's New: Kit's Latest Features That Make a Real Difference
Kit doesn't sit still. The team ships meaningful updates regularly, and their recent releases show they're listening to creators closely.
Kit MCP (Model Context Protocol):

This is a big one. Kit introduced MCP integration, which means you can connect AI tools directly to your Kit account. Imagine feeding your subscriber data and email performance into an AI assistant that drafts personalized email copy based on each segment's behavior. It's early days for this feature, but the potential is enormous — and it's available on the Creator plan right now.
Refined Commerce Dashboard:

Selling digital products through Kit just got cleaner. The updated commerce dashboard gives you real-time revenue tracking, subscription management, and customer insights all in one place. No more toggling between your email platform and a separate payment processor to figure out which email drove which sale.
Improved Subscriber Profiles:

Kit now shows richer subscriber data at a glance — purchase history, automation progress, engagement score, and custom field values — all on a single profile page. When you're writing a targeted email and want to understand who you're talking to, this view is gold.
Free Plan Upgrades:

The free Newsletter plan now includes one basic visual automation, unlimited landing pages and forms, unlimited broadcasts, and full audience tagging and segmentation. For someone just starting out, that's a serious toolkit at zero cost.
If you want the full rundown on every recent release, check our
Kit (ConvertKit) new features roundup
And if you're ready to experience these features hands-on,
start your free 14-day trial of Kit's Creator plan right here
. No credit card required. Free migration from your current platform included.
Who Is Kit Ideal For?
Kit isn't for every business. And honestly, that's a strength, not a weakness. Here's who gets the most out of it:
Writers and bloggers

who send regular newsletters and want clean, distraction-free email composition. Kit's editor is markdown-friendly and produces emails that look great in every inbox without clunky drag-and-drop formatting issues.
Course creators and coaches
who need automated sequences that nurture leads over days or weeks before presenting an offer. The visual automation builder was made for this exact workflow.
Podcasters and YouTubers

who want to move their audience off rented platforms (Spotify, YouTube) and onto a channel they own. Kit's landing pages make it easy to create a "join the community" page you can link from show notes or video descriptions.
Authors launching books
who need pre-launch sequences, launch-day blasts, and post-launch follow-ups — all triggered automatically based on subscriber behavior.
Musicians, artists, and creatives
who sell digital products, prints, presets, or memberships directly to fans. Kit's integrated commerce tools mean you can sell without Shopify or Gumroad.
If you fall into any of these categories and you've been wrestling with a generic email tool, Kit will feel like putting on glasses for the first time. Everything just… makes more sense.
Still deciding? Our comparison guide on
why to choose Kit (ConvertKit)
breaks down the decision further.
Tips for Getting the Most Out of Kit Starting Today
Here are concrete, actionable tips to hit the ground running:
Start with the free plan.
Seriously. Don't overthink it. Sign up, create one landing page, and drive traffic to it. You can upgrade later when you need automations or sequences.
Build a simple 5-email welcome sequence first.

Email 1: deliver the lead magnet + introduce yourself. Email 2: share your story. Email 3: teach something valuable. Email 4: social proof (testimonials, results). Email 5: soft pitch for your product or ask a question to encourage replies. This sequence alone can do more for your business than months of social media posting.
Use tags like a librarian, not a hoarder.

Create a naming convention early. Something like `source:instagram`, `interest:email-marketing`, `purchased:course-v1`. Future you will be grateful when you need to segment a campaign six months from now.
Set up a "cold subscriber" cleanup automation.

Kit tracks engagement. Create an automation that identifies subscribers who haven't opened an email in 90 days, sends them a re-engagement email, and unsubscribes them if they don't respond. A smaller, engaged list always outperforms a bloated, disengaged one.
Connect Kit to your other tools.
Kit integrates natively with dozens of platforms — WordPress, Shopify, Zapier, Teachable, and more. If you use Descript for podcast editing, you can even
connect your workflow with Kit
to automate show-notes-to-email pipelines. And if you're exploring AI-powered content creation tools, learn how
Gamma pairs well with Kit
for lead magnet design, or how
ElevenLabs can enhance your audio emails
Check the broadcast analytics weekly.
Kit gives you open rates, click rates, and subscriber growth per email. Look for patterns. Which subject lines get opened? Which links get clicked? Double down on what works.
The Bottom Line: Kit Lets You Focus on Creating
The best email platform is the one that gets out of your way and lets you do the work that matters — creating content, building relationships, and serving your audience. Kit does exactly that.

It's trusted by creators like James Clear, Ali Abdaal, Anne-Laure Le Cunff, and thousands of others who treat their email list as a core part of their business. The free plan is genuinely useful. The Creator plan unlocks everything you need to automate, segment, and monetize. And the 14-day trial lets you test every feature with zero risk.
If you've been putting off building your email list — or if you're stuck on a platform that wasn't designed for creators — this is the nudge you needed.
Start your free Kit trial today
and see why so many creators call it the best decision they made for their business.

children.text: Is Kit (ConvertKit) really free for up to 1,000 subscribers?

Yes. Kit's Newsletter plan is free for up to 1,000 subscribers and includes unlimited landing pages, unlimited email broadcasts, audience tagging, segmentation, one basic visual automation, and the ability to sell digital products. You can upgrade to the Creator plan anytime for unlimited automations, sequences, A/B testing, and more.
Can I migrate my existing email list to Kit from another platform?

Absolutely. Kit offers free migrations for new users. Their team handles the transfer of your subscribers, automations, and forms so you can switch without losing data or starting from scratch. During your 14-day free trial, migration support is included.
How is Kit different from Mailchimp or other general email tools?

Kit was built specifically for creators — writers, podcasters, course builders, and artists — rather than e-commerce stores or enterprise teams. Its subscriber-centric tagging system charges you per unique subscriber (not per list), the visual automation builder is intuitive and creator-focused, and you can sell digital products directly through the platform without third-party integrations.
